gene_region.label: COMT transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical COMT genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: COMT encodes catechol-O-methyltransferase, an enzyme that methylates catecholamines and catechol estrogens, linking neurotransmitter turnover, pain, stress, cognition, and pharmacogenetic research.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local COMT bundle is neurotransmitter-metabolism oriented. Val158Met is a common functional marker but remains a probabilistic research modifier, not a deterministic behavior or psychiatric predictor.
variant_effect_overview: Val158Met alters COMT enzyme thermostability/activity and is widely studied in dopamine, pain, stress, cognition, and medication-response contexts.
Interpretation should account for sex, tissue isoform, phenotype definition, ancestry, and environment.
condition_research_overview: Catecholamine metabolism and prefrontal dopamine tone
Pain, stress, and cognition association research
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: COMT methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for COMT.
Because COMT biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use COMT promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use.
Catecholamine metabolism and prefrontal dopamine tone
Pain, stress, and cognition association research

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000093010: COMT coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: COMT biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a COMT catecholamine-metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal signaling, pain/stress traits, methyl-donor use, or pharmacogenetic research. It should not be treated as a deterministic personality or psychiatric call.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs4680 (COMT Val158Met) is curated as a COMT marker for Catecholamine metabolism and prefrontal dopamine tone, Pain, stress, and cognition association research. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Catecholamine metabolism and prefrontal dopamine tone
Pain, stress, and cognition association research
research_context: Use this marker as a COMT pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs4680 (COMT Val158Met) with COMT methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs4680 (COMT Val158Met) or linked COMT locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Catecholamine metabolism and prefrontal dopamine tone, Pain, stress, and cognition association research
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs4680 (COMT Val158Met) as a gene-specific marker for Catecholamine metabolism and prefrontal dopamine tone, Pain, stress, and cognition association research and catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use.
